message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): timeout_with_cx observes budget deadline (tick 383)

Parallel to tick 382's sleep_with_cx budget test. Added
`timeout_with_cx_observes_budget_deadline` — pins the tick-328 doc
comment claim that the primitive observes cx budget deadline via
`asupersync::time::budget_timeout`.

Setup: cx with Budget::with_deadline(Time::ZERO) (already elapsed).
Call timeout_with_cx(cx, 30s, std::future::pending()). The pending
future never resolves, so without budget observation the call would
wait the full 30 seconds. With budget observation, returns Err
promptly.

Both tick-328 and tick-331 doc claims now have passing tests:
  - tick 382: sleep_with_cx budget observation pinned.
  - tick 383: timeout_with_cx budget observation pinned.

A regression removing budget_* short-circuit logic in asupersync
would now fire at least two tests, not just silently slip past.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 12s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
